places.name: Dolac — central farmers' market
places.description: Zagreb's terraced open-air market above Trg Bana Jelačića, with red umbrellas, produce halls, dairy, flowers, and the city's most concentrated morning food energy.
places.local_story: Generations have bought cheese, štrukli, seasonal fruit, and cured meats here before work. Locals call it the belly of Zagreb: noisy, fragrant, and unapologetically practical—you shop fast, greet vendors you know, and leave with bags that smell like the countryside.
places.practical_tip: Arrive before 9:00 on weekends for the calmest stalls and best choice; carry small cash for older sellers. Walk the upper terrace first for produce, then dip into the covered halls for dairy and meat.
highlights: Direct link to the Upper Town

places.name: Vincek — Ilica pastry institution
places.description: A beloved Zagreb patisserie on Ilica known for cakes, kremšnite, strudels, and ice cream—queue-at-the-door famous for generations.
places.local_story: Birthdays, name days, and 'just because' stops revolve around Vincek's display cases. It is less a trendy café than a ritual: point at a slice, take it to go, or squeeze in standing room when the line spills onto the sidewalk.
places.practical_tip: If the Ilica shop is packed, decide before you reach the counter—staff move quickly. Try kremšnita or a seasonal strudel; takeaway is normal.
highlights: Late hours for a pastry shop

places.name: Burek Hrama — late-night burek legend
places.description: A compact takeaway near the Cathedral serving hot burek, sirnica, and related bakes—open when bars close and locals need something salty.
places.local_story: After concerts and kafići, the smell of layered pastry pulls people off Petrinjska. It is not fine dining; it is fuel and comfort, eaten on a bench or walking toward the tram.
places.practical_tip: Order by name (burek mesni, sirnica, zeljanica); ask for yogurt if you want the classic pairing. Expect a queue after midnight on weekends.

places.name: La Štruk — štrukli house (Skalinska)
places.description: A cozy restaurant on steep Skalinska specializing in štrukli—baked or boiled filled dough from Hrvatsko zagorje—served in sweet and savory forms.
places.local_story: Štrukli migrated from Zagorje kitchens into Zagreb's identity; here they are the whole menu. Locals bring visitors when they want to explain 'what we actually eat' without white tablecloth fuss.
places.practical_tip: Book or arrive early for dinner; try classic cheese štrukli baked, then a seasonal sweet version. Pair with a light wine or beer—portions are rich.
highlights: Traditional Hrvatsko zagorje dish in the center

places.name: Tržnica Kvatric — neighborhood market hall
places.description: A large covered market building on Heinzelova with meat, fish, produce, and deli counters—where residential Zagreb does weekly shopping away from the old core.
places.local_story: Kvatric is everyday Zagreb: rolling bags, stall banter, and seasonal switches (wild asparagus, autumn mushrooms) without the postcard framing of Dolac. Food people come here for serious ingredients and lower tourist density.
places.practical_tip: Go weekday morning for the fullest fish and meat selection; bring a tote. The surrounding blocks have bakeries and small eateries if you want lunch after shopping.
